name: Shravanthi Murugesan

education.department: Manning College of Information and Computer Sciences
education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
coursework: Theory and Practice of Software Engineering
Research Methods in Empirical Computer Science

education.school: Sri Sivasubramaniya Nadar College of Engineering

experience.title: Extern
bullets: Working on fine-grained image captioning capabilities with a focus on factual accuracy and reducing hallucination in multimodal LLM outputs.
Extended a multi-agent LLM-MLLM verification pipeline to improve factual accuracy and reduce hallucination in image captioning at the attribute level.
Integrated GRPO-based reinforcement learning to replace multi-pass proposition verification with a single-pass approach, reducing latency and computational overhead.
Ran controlled experiments and analyzed failure cases across captioning benchmarks to measure gains in descriptive accuracy and efficiency.

experience.title: Research Assistant
bullets: Implemented a baseline model for edge detection in canine ultrasound videos using PyTorch.
Conducted a literature review on edge detection techniques and explored self-supervised approaches to address limited annotated data.

projects.name: One-shot Custom Stylization
projects.description: Modified GAN architecture for high-fidelity style transfer from a single reference image to unseen target domains
bullets: Developed a modified GAN architecture in PyTorch capable of performing high-fidelity style transfer from a single reference image to unseen target domains, with a focus on discriminator accuracy.
Created a user-friendly interface using Gradio allowing real-time user testing and style parameter adjustment.

projects.name: One-shot Custom Stylization (alt)
projects.description: Modified GAN architecture for high-fidelity style transfer from a single reference image to unseen target domains
bullets: Developed a modified GAN architecture in PyTorch capable of performing high-fidelity style transfer from a single reference image to unseen target domains, with a focus on discriminator accuracy.
Created a user-friendly interface using Gradio allowing real-time user testing and style parameter adjustment.

projects.name: Domain Generalization Across Distribution Shifts
projects.description: Learning framework to maintain prediction stability under distribution shifts including color, texture, and lighting variations
bullets: Implemented and replicated a learning framework to maintain prediction stability under distribution shifts including color, texture, and lighting variations, building training and evaluation pipelines.
Ran controlled domain-shift experiments and analyzed failure cases to identify which components contributed to improved stability over standard ERM baselines.

projects.name: Domain Generalization Across Distribution Shifts (alt)
projects.description: Learning framework to maintain prediction stability under distribution shifts including color, texture, and lighting variations
bullets: Implemented and replicated a learning framework to maintain prediction stability under distribution shifts including color, texture, and lighting variations, building training and evaluation pipelines.

projects.name: Semi-Supervised Hybrid Model for Advanced Few-Shot Learning
projects.description: Hybrid few-shot learning approach combining Semi-Supervised Learning and Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning
bullets: Developed a hybrid few-shot learning approach combining Semi-Supervised Learning and Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning.
Utilized a pre-trained model to generate pseudo-labels and combined with MAML training process.

projects.name: Semi-Supervised Hybrid Model for Advanced Few-Shot Learning (alt)
projects.description: Hybrid few-shot learning approach combining Semi-Supervised Learning and Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning
bullets: Developed a hybrid few-shot learning approach combining Semi-Supervised Learning and Model-Agnostic Meta-Learning.

projects.name: Fine-grained Image Captioning with LLM-MLLM Verification
projects.description: Multi-agent LLM-MLLM pipeline to improve factual accuracy and reduce hallucination in image captioning at the attribute level
bullets: Extended a multi-agent LLM-MLLM verification pipeline to improve factual accuracy and reduce hallucination in image captioning at the attribute level.
Integrated GRPO-based reinforcement learning to replace multi-pass proposition verification with a single-pass approach, reducing latency and computational overhead.
Ran controlled experiments and analyzed failure cases across captioning benchmarks to measure gains in descriptive accuracy and efficiency.

Ran controlled experiments and analyzed failure cases across captioning benchmarks to measure gains in descriptive accuracy and efficiency.

projects.name: Edge Detection in Canine Ultrasound Videos
projects.description: Baseline model for edge detection in canine ultrasound videos using PyTorch with self-supervised approaches
bullets: Implemented a baseline model for edge detection in canine ultrasound videos using PyTorch.
Conducted a literature review on edge detection techniques and explored self-supervised approaches to address limited annotated data.

projects.name: Meal Planner
projects.description: Full-stack meal planning web app using React and external recipe APIs
bullets: Built a full-stack meal planning platform using React that generates a weekly meal plan using Spoonacular and Fat Secret APIs.
Implemented user authentication, dynamic shopping lists, and responsive RESTful services for recipe and health data.

projects.name: Meal Planner (alt)
projects.description: Full-stack meal planning web app using React and external recipe APIs
bullets: Built a full-stack meal planning platform using React that generates a weekly meal plan using Spoonacular and Fat Secret APIs.
Implemented user authentication, dynamic shopping lists, and responsive RESTful services for recipe and health data.

projects.name: Automated Detection of Epilepsy Using EEG Signals
projects.description: Deep learning model for epilepsy seizure detection from EEG signals achieving ~87% accuracy
bullets: Built a deep learning model for non-reconstructive seizure detection from EEG signals, achieving ~87% accuracy and ~0.80 AUC.
Compared LSTM, decision tree, and rule-based approaches; optimized for high-recall detection of epileptogenic zones.
Trained and evaluated on a public EEG dataset, achieving ~94% validation accuracy with optimization.

projects.name: Automated Detection of Epilepsy Using EEG Signals (alt)
projects.description: Deep learning model for epilepsy seizure detection from EEG signals achieving ~87% accuracy
bullets: Built a deep learning model for non-reconstructive seizure detection from EEG signals, achieving ~87% accuracy and ~0.80 AUC.
Compared LSTM, decision tree, and rule-based approaches; optimized for high-recall detection of epileptogenic zones.
Trained and evaluated on a public EEG dataset, achieving ~94% validation accuracy with optimization.

projects.name: RobotApp
projects.description: End-to-end autonomous robot navigation system using ROS, LiDAR, and RGB-D sensing
bullets: Built an end-to-end autonomous robot navigation system using ROS with LiDAR and RGB-D sensing for spatial anomaly detection.
Implemented goal-directed navigation with depth estimation, loop closure, and random waypoint logic.
Evaluated robustness across noisy environments and behavior under sensor noise conditions.

projects.description: End-to-end autonomous robot navigation system using ROS, LiDAR, and RGB-D sensing
